text:
GUARDANT360 LIQUID BIOPSY / CIRCULATING TUMOR DNA NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
PATIENT AND SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Patient Name:             Margaret Yuen
Date of Birth:            [ON FILE]
Medical Record Number:    [ON FILE]
Ordering Physician:       [Treating Oncologist]
Institution:              [Cancer Center / Outpatient Oncology Clinic]
Specimen Type:            Peripheral blood (plasma, cell-free DNA)
Specimen Collection:      Peripheral venipuncture, two K2-EDTA tubes (20 mL total)
Specimen Received:        [DATE]
Report Issued:            [DATE]
Clinical Indication:      Non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma (EGFR L858R-mutant, Stage IIIB at diagnosis), disease progression on first-line osimertinib (Tagrisso 80 mg daily); evaluation for acquired resistance mechanisms

Assay Platform:           Guardant360 (Guardant Health, Inc.)
Panel:                    74-gene hybrid capture cell-free DNA panel with digital error suppression
Sequencing Technology:    Massively parallel sequencing (next-generation sequencing) of plasma-derived cell-free DNA (cfDNA)
Analytical Sensitivity:   Variant allele fraction (VAF) as low as 0.1% for point mutations (gene- and variant-dependent)
Tumor Mutational Burden:  Estimated from circulating tumor DNA (low; see below)
MSI Status:               Microsatellite stable (MSS)

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
CLINICAL SUMMARY
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Ms. Yuen is a 63-year-old Chinese-American female with a known history of Stage IIIB pulmonary adenocarcinoma (right upper lobe; T3N2M0, AJCC 8th edition) harboring an EGFR exon 21 L858R sensitizing point mutation, initially identified on tissue-based NGS and confirmed by PCR at the time of diagnosis. She was treated with first-line osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally once daily (initiated approximately 6 weeks following diagnosis), achieving a significant partial response by RECIST 1.1 criteria (primary mass reduction from 4.8 cm to a nadir of 1.8 cm; normalization of mediastinal lymphadenopathy) over the first 9 months of therapy.

Approximately 14 months after initiating osimertinib, restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and concurrent MRI brain with gadolinium demonstrated systemic and intracranial disease progression: the right upper lobe primary mass increased from 1.8 cm (nadir) to 3.2 cm, new right paratracheal lymphadenopathy was identified (1.4 cm), a new small right pleural effusion was noted, and two new enhancing brain lesions were identified (1.1 cm right frontal lobe; 0.8 cm right cerebellar hemisphere), consistent with brain metastases.

This liquid biopsy was ordered to characterize the molecular resistance landscape, specifically to evaluate for the presence of the EGFR T790M resistance mutation and other mechanisms of acquired resistance to third-generation EGFR TKI therapy.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
GENOMIC FINDINGS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

--- SECTION 1: ACTIONABLE ALTERATIONS ---

1. EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G), Exon 21 | VAF: 3.8%
   Classification: SENSITIZING MUTATION вЂ” Tier I (Strong clinical significance)
   Gene Function: Epidermal growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase
   Interpretation: The EGFR L858R point mutation is the canonical sensitizing alteration in NSCLC, accounting for approximately 40-45% of all EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinomas. Its detection in circulating tumor DNA at VAF 3.8% confirms continued shedding of EGFR-mutant tumor DNA into the plasma and is consistent with active disease. This mutation confers sensitivity to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs). Of note, the relatively modest VAF in plasma likely reflects the dynamics of tumor shedding at the time of blood draw relative to total cfDNA input, rather than a low tumor burden signal per se, given the radiographically confirmed progressive disease.

2. EGFR p.T790M (c.2369C>T), Exon 20 | VAF: 1.6%
   Classification: ACQUIRED RESISTANCE MUTATION вЂ” Tier I (Strong clinical significance)
   Gene Function: Epidermal growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase
   Interpretation: The EGFR T790M substitution introduces a methionine at the gatekeeper position of the EGFR kinase domain (threonine 790), sterically impeding the binding of first- and second-generation EGFR TKIs (erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib, dacomitinib) and conferring resistance. In the canonical clinical paradigm, T790M arises as an acquired resistance mutation in approximately 50-60% of patients who progress on first- or second-generation EGFR TKIs and is targetable by osimertinib (Tagrisso). However, in the present clinical context, this patient received osimertinib as her initial (first-line) EGFR-directed therapy, and its appearance here is atypical and clinically noteworthy.

   The detection of T790M at relatively low VAF (1.6%) in the context of upfront osimertinib progression is subject to two leading interpretations:

   (a) Pre-existing subclonal T790M population: A small T790M-mutant subclone may have been present below the detection limit of baseline tissue or liquid biopsy testing at the time of diagnosis. Under the selective pressure of osimertinib (which, although potent against T790M-positive cells, may exert differential clonal pressure across heterogeneous tumor subpopulations), this subclone may have expanded to detectable levels. This mechanism has been described in a subset of osimertinib-treated patients, particularly those who progress relatively early (within 12-18 months).

   (b) De novo T790M emergence / concurrent bypass mechanism: T790M may represent an independent resistance event arising in the context of molecular tumor evolution under osimertinib pressure. In this scenario, T790M co-exists with other bypass track resistance mechanisms (e.g., MET amplification, ERBB2 amplification, BRAF alterations) that may be the primary drivers of clinical resistance, with T790M representing a secondary or parallel mechanism. Given the relatively low VAF (1.6%) compared to L858R (3.8%), the T790M-positive subclone does not appear to be the dominant circulating clone at this time point.

   Neither osimertinib continuation nor re-challenge with first-/second-generation EGFR TKIs would be expected to provide durable benefit in this setting. The detection of T790M in a patient progressing on osimertinib does not meet the standard clinical indication for osimertinib initiation (since the patient is already receiving this agent). This finding, in combination with the absence of other currently detected bypass mechanisms in this plasma specimen, will inform multi-disciplinary discussion regarding subsequent therapeutic options.

   IMPORTANT NOTE: Liquid biopsy has known false-negative limitations, particularly for copy number alterations (e.g., MET amplification, ERBB2 amplification) which may not be reliably detected in plasma-based assays. Tissue re-biopsy should be considered to comprehensively evaluate for bypass resistance mechanisms that may be underrepresented in ctDNA.

--- SECTION 2: CO-MUTATIONS AND SECONDARY ALTERATIONS ---

3. TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T), Exon 7 | VAF: 5.2%
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tier II
   Gene Function: Tumor suppressor; transcription factor regulating cell cycle arrest, apoptosis, and DNA damage response
   Interpretation: TP53 R248W is a well-characterized hotspot gain-of-function missense mutation at codon 248 (one of the most frequently mutated residues in human cancer), resulting in loss of wild-type tumor suppressive function and acquisition of oncogenic gain-of-function activities including promotion of invasion, resistance to apoptosis, and altered metabolic reprogramming. This mutation was previously identified in the patient's original tissue-based NGS at VAF 38% and is now detected in plasma at VAF 5.2%, consistent with continued clonal persistence. TP53 mutations are frequent co-mutations in EGFR-mutant NSCLC and have been associated with shorter progression-free survival on EGFR TKI therapy in some studies, though they are not independently targetable with currently approved agents. VAF ratio of TP53:EGFR L858R (5.2%:3.8% в‰€ 1.4) is approximately consistent with co-occurrence in the same tumor clone, as expected.

4. ARID1A frameshift mutation (specific position: [VAF 0.9%])
   Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS) вЂ” Tier III
   Gene Function: SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex; tumor suppressor
   Interpretation: An ARID1A frameshift mutation is detected at very low VAF (0.9%), near the lower analytical sensitivity threshold of this assay. ARID1A was previously identified as a VUS (p.E1814*, VAF 18%) in the original tissue-based NGS. The low VAF in plasma may reflect a minor subclone or represent stochastic sampling variation in cfDNA. ARID1A loss-of-function has been described in a subset of lung adenocarcinomas and has been associated with increased sensitivity to immune checkpoint blockade in some tumor types, though clinical significance in this setting remains investigational.

--- SECTION 3: COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS ---

5. MET copy number: WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS (no amplification detected)
   Interpretation: MET amplification is one of the most clinically significant bypass resistance mechanisms to osimertinib, reported in approximately 15-26% of patients with acquired resistance to third-generation EGFR TKIs in tissue-based studies. The absence of detectable MET amplification by plasma ctDNA does NOT exclude MET amplification as a resistance mechanism, as copy number alterations are generally less sensitively detected in liquid biopsy compared to tissue NGS (particularly at lower levels of amplification). Tissue re-biopsy with dedicated FISH or tissue-based comprehensive genomic profiling is strongly recommended to definitively assess MET amplification status.

6. ERBB2 (HER2) copy number: WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS (no amplification detected)
   Interpretation: ERBB2 amplification is another described mechanism of acquired resistance to EGFR TKI therapy. As with MET, absence of detection in plasma does not exclude tissue-level ERBB2 amplification. No ERBB2 exon 20 insertion was detected.

7. FGFR1 copy number: WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS (no amplification detected)

--- SECTION 4: GENE FUSIONS AND STRUCTURAL REARRANGEMENTS ---

8. ALK fusions: NOT DETECTED
9. ROS1 fusions: NOT DETECTED
10. RET fusions: NOT DETECTED
11. NTRK1/2/3 fusions: NOT DETECTED
12. MET exon 14 splice site mutations: NOT DETECTED

--- SECTION 5: ADDITIONAL GENES EVALUATED (SELECTED REPORTABLE FINDINGS) ---

BRAF V600E: NOT DETECTED
BRAF non-V600 mutations: NOT DETECTED
KRAS: NOT DETECTED (consistent with EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma biology; EGFR and KRAS mutations are nearly mutually exclusive)
EGFR exon 19 deletion: NOT DETECTED
EGFR exon 20 insertion: NOT DETECTED
EGFR p.T790M: DETECTED (see above)
EGFR p.C797S (exon 20): NOT DETECTED вЂ” This is clinically significant; the absence of the C797S gatekeeper resistance mutation argues against a triple-mutant (L858R/T790M/C797S) resistance mechanism, which would be relevant to the potential activity of subsequent EGFR-directed therapies
EGFR p.G719X, p.S768I, p.L861Q (other sensitizing mutations): NOT DETECTED
PIK3CA: NOT DETECTED
NRAS: NOT DETECTED
HRAS: NOT DETECTED

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB) вЂ” LIQUID BIOPSY ESTIMATE
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Estimated TMB (plasma-derived): LOW
Note: TMB estimation from plasma ctDNA is an approximation and subject to greater variability than tissue-based TMB measurement. The low estimated TMB is directionally consistent with the tissue-based TMB of 6.2 mut/Mb measured at diagnosis. Low TMB in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is expected given the driver mutation biology and the typically non-hypermutated nature of EGFR-mutant tumors. This finding does not support monotherapy immune checkpoint inhibitor use as a primary strategy in this setting, consistent with established data demonstrating limited benefit (and potential increased toxicity) of PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor monotherapy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Interpretation: MSS status is consistent with the prior molecular characterization and is expected in EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma. This finding does not support the use of MSI-H-directed checkpoint inhibitor therapy (e.g., pembrolizumab per MSI-H indication).

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
CLINICAL INTERPRETATION AND SUMMARY
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

This Guardant360 plasma ctDNA analysis performed at disease progression on upfront osimertinib reveals the following key findings:

1. EGFR L858R confirmed (VAF 3.8%): The founding sensitizing driver mutation remains detectable in circulating tumor DNA, confirming continued EGFR-mutant tumor biology.

2. EGFR T790M detected (VAF 1.6%): An acquired resistance mutation is identified at low VAF. In the context of upfront (first-line) osimertinib therapy вЂ” a third-generation TKI designed to overcome T790M resistance вЂ” the emergence of T790M is clinically atypical and mechanistically complex. The low VAF relative to the founding L858R clone raises the question of whether this reflects a minor pre-existing subclone selected for under osimertinib pressure, rather than the dominant mechanism of clinical resistance. The absence of the EGFR C797S mutation rules out the triple-mutant resistance configuration.

3. No MET amplification, ERBB2 amplification, or FGFR1 amplification detected in plasma: These are the most clinically relevant bypass track resistance mechanisms to osimertinib and are not detected in this plasma specimen. However, the sensitivity of liquid biopsy for copy number amplifications is significantly lower than for point mutations and SNVs, and a false-negative result for MET or ERBB2 amplification cannot be excluded. Given that MET amplification is one of the most common mechanisms of acquired osimertinib resistance and has direct therapeutic implications (including for EGFR/MET bispecific antibody strategies under investigation), tissue re-biopsy with comprehensive genomic profiling (e.g., FoundationOne CDx, tissue-based hybrid capture panel with robust CNV detection) is strongly recommended.

4. No EGFR C797S: The absence of C797S in the context of T790M means that allosteric EGFR inhibition strategies (e.g., osimertinib re-challenge in combination with first-generation TKIs in the cis-C797S/T790M configuration) would not be precluded on allele-configuration grounds; however, the clinical utility of such combinations in this scenario remains investigational.

5. TP53 R248W confirmed as a persistent co-mutation (VAF 5.2%): No new clinically actionable alterations identified.

Conclusion: Acquired EGFR T790M resistance mutation confirmed on liquid biopsy in the setting of disease progression on third-generation EGFR TKI (osimertinib). This finding is atypical in this context and likely reflects a pre-existing subclonal T790M population enriched under osimertinib selective pressure, potentially in concert with undetected bypass mechanisms. The treating team is advised to pursue tissue re-biopsy of an accessible progressing lesion for comprehensive genomic profiling to fully characterize the resistance landscape and guide selection among potential subsequent therapies, which may include amivantamab (EGFR/MET bispecific antibody) plus lazertinib, platinum-based chemotherapy doublet, or appropriately matched clinical trial enrollment.

ASSAY QUALITY METRICS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Total cfDNA input:               Adequate for analysis
Unique molecule depth (coverage): Sufficient for all reportable genes (>500x unique depth across covered regions)
ctDNA fraction estimate:         Low (consistent with the clinical and biological context)
Specimen quality:                PASS вЂ” all internal quality controls met
Analysis method:                 Digital error suppression (proprietary Guardant Health algorithm); background error modeling for low-VAF variant calling

Note on ctDNA fraction: The overall ctDNA fraction is low in this specimen (reflected in the relatively modest VAFs for all detected alterations). This is not uncommon in the clinical setting of plasma sampling during active TKI therapy or with lower-volume disease, and does not preclude interpretation of the above findings. However, it does increase the possibility of false-negative results for lower-abundance alterations (including copy number gains/amplifications and certain structural rearrangements).
